title: Senate Minority Leader Brian W. Jones ( R-San Diego ) is sounding the alarm on a looming energy and economic crisis, warning that California gas prices could soar 75% to $8.43 per
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California gas prices could soar 75% to $8.43 per gallon in 2026 due to shutdowns of two major in-state refineries.",
    "The Phillips 66 refinery in Los Angeles is slated to shut down by the end of 2025.",
    "The Valero refinery in Benicia is expected to close in April 2026.",
    "The two refineries produce approximately 20% of California’s in-state gasoline supply.",
    "A recent analysis by USC Professor Michael Mische projects that gas prices could surge 75% by the end of 2026 because of the refinery closures.",
    "Michael Mische's models estimate a jump to $6.43 per gallon by late 2025 following the Phillips 66 closure.",
    "Gas prices could reach as high as $8.43 in 2026 after the Valero refinery shuts down.",
    "The two refineries directly employ 1,300 Californians and support nearly 3,000 jobs statewide using the commonly accepted job multiplier for the industry at 2.3.",
    "The loss of these refineries will destabilize fuel supply and drive gas prices through the roof.",
    "California's excessive regulations and financial burdens on gasoline producers have made it increasingly difficult for refineries to remain operational."
  ]
}
```
